STATE UNIVERSITIES ANNUITANTS ASSOCIATION AND FOUNDATION
Servingretirees, spouses and SUNivors from the pUblicUniversfties
and CommunityCollegesof Illinois
30B5 Stevenson Drive· Suite 301. Springfield, IL 62703
Telephone (217) 585-2370' (888) 547-8473· Fax (217) 585-2372
a-mail: suaa@suaa.org·website:WWN.su88.oro
EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR February 14, 2007
Don Naylor, CAE
Dear Colleagues,
EXECUTIVECOMMITTEE State universities and community college retirees and employees face increasing threats to
their pension and health benefits. The State of Illinois continues to face a structural deficit
President: problem. The qovemor's recent push to sell the lottery system for an estimated $10 billion is
Teny M.Weidner (EIU)
clear evidence of the problem. Rumors suggest that SURS will see yet a third year of
44 Heather Drive
Charleston, n, 61920 underfunding.
(217) 345-5320
SUAA took action several years ago to address challenges to the current system of funding
tmweidner@eiu.edu
your pensions commonly referred to as defined benefits plans. After several years of
preparation SUAA created SUAACTION, a political involvement committee to become
Past-President:
politically active. In the past it was possible to successfully advocate legislatively through
David Hilquist (OCC)
1 Cayuga Court lobbying. This is no longer the case since politics in Illinois has become a pay to play game.
HawthorneWoods,IL 60047
To address these challenges to both active and retired SURS members, SUAA in 2005
(847) 823-2214
established SUAACTION. SUAACTION will serve as the political advocacy ann of SUAA.
dhilqUis@oakton.edu
Through SUAACTION, SUM will maintain a permanent political presence in Springfield in
Treasurer: addition to lobbying in the traditional manner. If successful our move will send a clear signal
Katharine KraJ (UIUC) to those wanting to continue to underfund your retirement system and reduce or eliminate
23 Long Grove Drive hard earned benefits.
Monticello,IL 61856
SUAACTION will focus its activities in four major areas: (1) securing full funding for SURS
(217) 782-2849
kral@monlicellopro.net in accordance with the 1995 pension law and oppose all efforts to further erode our pension
benefits; (2) promoting the highest ethical and professional standards in the management of
Membe'-at-Large: SURS assets; (3) preserving the health benefits we now enjoy; (4) expanding efforts to
Burton Witthuhn (WIU) eliminate Social Security penalties, the Windfall Elimination Provision (WEP) and
1106Bayberry Lane Government Pension Offset (GPO).
Macomb,IL 61455
(309) 833-1939 The SUAACTION political advocacy program wilt be expensive, and dues cannot be used
tacsezaemsn.con
for political purposes since SUAA is a non-profit, bi-partisan organization. SUAACTION has
recommended that its activities be financed by voluntary contributions. Will you 'Volunteer?
Member-at-Large:
AlanVoelker(NIU) If SUAACTIQN is to be successful, it needs your support. If you have not yet contributed to
1505Margaret Lane SUAACTION, please do so now by using the form below. If you have already made a
DeKalb, IL 60115 contribution, we thank you for your support. Additional contributions are always welcome
815'756-7447
avoelker@niu.edu Thank you in advance for helping us move forward.
Member...at-Large: SUAACTION CONTRIBUTION
Rance Thomas (LCC)
Please send directly to: SUAA (Make check payable to SUAACTIONj
2480Wedgwood Drive West
Florissant, MO 63033
3085 Stevenson Drive, Suite 301
(314) 921-7364
rthomas257@aol.com
Springfield, lL 62703
Member-at-Large:
Enclosed is my voluntary contribution to protect my retirement benefits.
JanetCook (ISU)
17438 EastWalden Road I am eontributing: $3o_, $25_, $20_, $15_, $10_, other amount __
Hudson,IL 61748
(309) 726-1664 Please print your name and address here:
jmcook@ilslu.edu

chunks.label: Facts

final_output:
## Document Type: Petition for Full Funding of State Universities Retirement System (SURS)

### Overview:
The State Universities Annuitants Association and Foundation (SUAA) has initiated efforts to secure full funding for the State Universities Retirement System (SURS). The SUAA also plans to set up a political advocacy arm, **SUAACTION**, with the objective of preventing any reductions or eliminations in pension benefits. 

### Main Content:
In response to the State of Illinois’ financial deficit, which could potentially affect all pension payouts including those managed by SURS, SUAA has taken proactive steps to ensure proper funding under current laws. They have established SUAACTION as a political advocacy arm to work alongside traditional lobbying efforts in Springfield.

SUAACTION's key objectives are:
- To secure full funding for SURS at the level set by 1995 pension laws.
- To resist any attempts to reduce or eliminate pension benefits.
- To promote ethical management practices.
- To end social security penalties often imposed on retirees, which include welfare dependency penalties (WEP) and poverty-related penalty (GPO).

To fund these efforts, SUAACTION plans to raise money through voluntary donations. At the current time, contributions should be directed towards SUAACTION’s specific account because existing contributors can continue the advocacy work.

### Key Takeaways:
- **SUAA** has developed a political arm, **SUAACTION**, to advocate for full funding of SURS and prevent benefits reductions.
- Objectives include securing pension law compliance and removing social security penalties faced by retirees.
- Contributions will be directed towards SUAACTION's account, ensuring the continuation of advocacy work.

### Flags:
- None

---

This structure adheres to the strict guidelines provided, merging all relevant parts into a single, coherent document.

terms.meaning: Pension plans where the amount of retirement benefit is predetermined
A long-term financial imbalance in a government's budget or accounts
A situation where political influence and lobbying are heavily dependent on contributions from interest groups
